cards.name: Nob Hill
cards.address: Nob Hill, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Nob Hill in Saratoga for a solid morning walk through the nature preserve. It's a great mix of open ridge views and shaded sections that never gets old on a cool morning like this.
cards.why: Cool temps and low rain odds make this a genuinely comfortable morning hike.

cards.name: Hong's Gourmet
cards.address: 14510 Big Basin Wy #9, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Hong's Gourmet on Big Basin Way in Saratoga for lunch. The setting is cozy and the Chinese food here punches well above its strip-mall address.
cards.why: You're already in Saratoga, so this is the natural lunch stop.

cards.name: American Cancer Society Discovery Shop - Los Altos, CA
cards.address: 243 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into the American Cancer Society Discovery Shop on Main Street in Los Altos. It's a proper thrift browse — clothing, housewares, books, and the occasional great find for next to nothing.
cards.why: Good thrifting takes time, and this place rewards a slow look around.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 125 S Frances St, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Stop by Philz Coffee.
cards.why: Swapped in as a fresh pick for this slot.

cards.name: Celebration of Women Art Exhibit
cards.blurb: View paintings celebrating women artists and attend a creative writing workshop inspired by the art.
cards.why: Free, walkable from downtown Los Altos, and a nice creative detour before dinner.

cards.name: Water Tower Kitchen
cards.address: 201 Orchard City Dr, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at Water Tower Kitchen in Campbell. American comfort food done well, good drinks, and a relaxed indoor vibe that fits a Thursday perfectly.
cards.why: Solid dinner with a crowd that isn't trying too hard.

Tonight's plan, locked in. 🔥

Bay Area Glass Institute in San Jose is hosting a free Community Night at 7 PM with visiting artist Penelope Rakov Lee. There's an artist talk, and then — live glassblowing. In person. Right in front of you.

If you've never watched someone pull molten glass into shape, it's one of those things that's hard to look away from. And Penelope Rakov Lee brings real depth to it — the talk gives you context, and then you get to watch the work actually happen.

Free. All welcome. No reason not to go.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee

cards.address: Guadalupe River Park

cards.name: Heroes
cards.address: 24 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Heroes, a comic and pop culture bookstore on Campbell Ave with a solid mix of graphic novels, back issues, and collectibles — easy to lose an hour in here.
cards.why: It's a fun, low-key morning stop with zero pressure to buy anything.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Head to the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum for real mummies, hieroglyphics, and artifacts you genuinely wouldn't expect to find in San Jose. The planetarium and garden grounds are worth a lap too.
cards.why: It's one of the most underrated places in the whole South Bay and way more interesting than it sounds.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: San Pedro Square Market is the move for lunch — grab a taco from one vendor, maybe ramen or banh mi from another, and find a patio seat to eat in the sun. Treat it like a food hall crawl.
cards.why: No commitment to a single menu, which is exactly what you want after a museum.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: The Computer History Museum is worth every minute. The main Revolution exhibit takes you from room-sized computers to the iPhone era and somehow makes all of it feel wild. Go at your own pace.
cards.why: The 71-degree afternoon is perfect for an indoor stretch, and this exhibit is genuinely hard to rush through.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the lakeside paths, watch the ducks, and unwind on the grass as the afternoon cools down.
cards.why: A peaceful outdoor wind-down between the museum and your evening massage fits the day's easy-going vibe perfectly.
